translation_text: The skilful traveller leaves no traces of his wheels or footsteps;
translation_text_raw: The skilful traveller leaves no traces of his wheels or footsteps;

chinese_text: 善言無瑕讁；善數不用籌策；
translation_text: the skilful speaker says nothing that can be found fault with or blamed; the skilful reckoner uses no tallies;
translation_text_raw: the skilful speaker says nothing that can be found fault with or blamed; the skilful reckoner uses no tallies;

translation_text: the skilful closer needs no bolts or bars, while to open what he has shut will be impossible;
translation_text_raw: the skilful closer needs no bolts or bars, while to open what he has shut will be impossible;

translation_text: the skilful binder uses no strings or knots, while to unloose what he has bound will be impossible.
translation_text_raw: the skilful binder uses no strings or knots, while to unloose what he has bound will be impossible.

chinese_text: 是以聖人常善救人，故無棄人；
translation_text: In the same way the sage is always skilful at saving men, and so he does not cast away any man;
translation_text_raw: In the same way the sage is always skilful at saving men, and so he does not cast away any man;

translation_text: he is always skilful at saving things, and so he does not cast away anything.
translation_text_raw: he is always skilful at saving things, and so he does not cast away anything.

translation_text: This is called 'Hiding the light of his procedure.'
translation_text_raw: This is called 'Hiding the light of his procedure.'

translation_text: Therefore the man of skill is a master (to be looked up to) by him who has not the skill;
translation_text_raw: Therefore the man of skill is a master (to be looked up to) by him who has not the skill;

translation_text: and he who has not the skill is the helper of (the reputation of) him who has the skill.
translation_text_raw: and he who has not the skill is the helper of (the reputation of) him who has the skill.

chinese_text: 不貴其師，不愛其資，雖智大迷，
translation_text: If the one did not honour his master, and the other did not rejoice in his helper, an (observer), though intelligent, might greatly err about them.
translation_text_raw: If the one did not honour his master, and the other did not rejoice in his helper, an (observer), though intelligent, might greatly err about them.

translation_text: This is called 'The utmost degree of mystery.'
translation_text_raw: This is called 'The utmost degree of mystery.'
